content.body: Each CARICOM nation has its own national treatment guidelines and essential medicines lists for antiretroviral therapy. However, there is significant alignment with the PAHO/WHO regional guidance. Barbados, Jamaica, and Trinidad & Tobago have well-established national HIV programs, while smaller islands like St. Lucia and Grenada rely more on the CARICOM Regional Standard.

Jamaica's national formulary includes all major ARV classes, while Guyana and Belize have more limited selections primarily of older, generic agents. Regular stock-outs remain a challenge across the region.
content.title: CARICOM Antiretroviral Formularies

content.definition: The CARICOM Regional Standard for HIV/AIDS treatment provides harmonized guidance on antiretroviral therapy, opportunistic infection management, and other aspects of care for the Caribbean region. It is a key resource for smaller island states without robust national guidelines.

content.body: Many Caribbean countries have implemented community-based adherence clubs, mobile clinics, and other patient engagement strategies to improve ART retention and viral suppression rates, which can be variable across the region.
content.title: Adherence Support Initiatives
